author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
e mana! tu baḍa kalira bhūta
kara bala jāri, śūnye diyā bāḍi,
hāsaye tapana⋅suta

bhūtera bāpera, śrāddha kara niti,
bhūtera vegāra khāṭa
lāja nāhi mukhe, kāla kāṭa’ sukhe,
calicha jamera bāṭa

kāminī⋅kāñcana, hṛdaya⋅rañjana,
tāhāte magana thāka
o dike tomāra, ki daśā ghaṭiche,
tāra kichu khoja rākha

caurāśī narake, jābe eke eke,
patha pariṣkāra prāya
kapālera jora, baḍa baṭe tora,
bāhādurī habe tāya

mūrakha barbvara, suyukati dhara,
jadi taribāre cāo
kahe premānande, manera ānande,
sadā hari-guṇa gāo
en_translation:
O my mind! You are a great ghost of the Age of Kali. You keep exerting useless force and striking out into the void, while the Son of the Sun-god laughs at you.

You always perform the rituals for the ghost's father and work as a forced laborer for ghosts. You have no shame; you pass your time happily while walking straight on the path to the God of Death.

You remain engrossed in women and gold, which delight your heart. On the other hand, do you keep any track of the terrible condition that is befalling you?

You will go to the eighty-four hells one by one—the path is almost clear. Your fate is truly strong; there will be great "heroism" in that.

O foolish barbarian! Adopt good reasoning if you wish to cross over this ocean. Premānanda says: "With a joyful mind, always sing the glories of Lord Hari."
